interpretation: At this high prior weight, the true lattice beats none and the spectrum-matched permutation, with smoother and more low-frequency learned latent temporal deltas on the true graph.

Oracle heat dynamics are accurate, but raw D_dX did not favor the true graph and lower lambda only partly reduced harm; graph-generated dynamics did not automatically translate into latent graph-prior utility.

Raw alignment strongly favors the true graph, but 5 epochs classifies as generic smoothing; at 20 epochs graph beats permuted, while at 50 epochs permuted catches up despite graph having smoother latent deltas.
At 5 epochs the true spring lattice is best at H=32 and the latent audit favors true-graph temporal smoothness.

graph_source: true spring lattice plus temporal_smooth baseline
interpretation: Calibrated temporal_smooth slightly beats graph at H32, so temporal smoothing is sufficient and graph topology is not necessary to explain the low-budget gain.
At 20 epochs no-prior is best at H=32; the true graph still beats permuted, but the graph prior no longer improves over no-prior.
At 5 epochs the true wave lattice is best at H=32 and latent deltas are smoother in the true-graph basis.

graph_source: true wave lattice plus temporal_smooth baseline
interpretation: Calibrated temporal_smooth beats graph at H32, so temporal smoothing is sufficient and graph gain is smoothness-like.
At 20 epochs no-prior is best at H=32; the true graph remains better than permuted but loses to the unconstrained GNN.
At 5 epochs the distance-kNN candidate graph is best at H=32 despite only approximating long-range interactions.
Graph beats both calibrated temporal_smooth and spectrum-matched permuted control at H32, supporting candidate topology-specific usefulness under the quick budget.
At 20 epochs graph beats no-prior but loses to the permuted graph, supporting generic smoothing rather than topology-specific attribution.

The correlation graph is smoother than permuted and random controls in raw temporal changes, but graph prior rollout is worse than both none and permuted; raw alignment did not guarantee rollout gain.
Smoke run only: graph beats no-prior slightly but loses to permuted; prefer T2000_train160 as the main METR-LA case.
